strengths: Use of concrete visual evidence (application screenshots and mentor notes) to illustrate communication, research, and collaboration ATL skills in action
Photographic documentation of peer interaction that clearly shows negotiation and feedback gathering
Description of self-management strategies through Gantt charts and monthly planning that reveal organization skills
weaknesses: Weekly schedule chart needs stronger alignment with long-term milestones to show cohesive self-management
Insufficient linkage between the use of digital art tools and specific learning progress or outcomes
Lack of a concrete example demonstrating how peer or teacher feedback was implemented to improve the product
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your application of ATL skills is well supported by visual artifacts that demonstrate communication, collaboration, and organizational strategies. To deepen this section, explicitly connect your weekly planning tools to overarching project milestones, and provide a concrete instance where feedback led to a tangible improvement in your work. Strengthening these links will showcase not only that you employed ATL skills, but also how they directly impacted your progress and the quality of your product.

strengths: Clear and detailed SMART analyses for both the learning goal and the product outcome, providing measurable milestones and relevance
Succinct articulation of the learning goal and intended product, which focuses and aligns the planning
Comprehensive Gantt chart demonstrating strong sequencing of tasks, deadlines, and resource allocation
weaknesses: Opening statement does not explicitly state a measurable learning goal, leading to initial ambiguity
Research source table and success-criteria rubric lack clear links to timelines, sequencing, and the tools or resources that will support each criterion
Post-exhibition action plan and task-table legend omit critical details on how feedback will inform revisions and the logic behind color-coding
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning section is anchored by well-constructed SMART analyses and a thorough Gantt chart, both of which give the project clear direction and measurable checkpoints. However, the early stages of the plan would benefit from a more explicit statement of the learning goal and tighter integration between your research sources, success criteria, and the tools or timelines that will drive their achievement. Providing a more detailed legend and reflection on how feedback will shape post-exhibition adjustments would enhance the feasibility and clarity of your plan.

strengths: Insightful recognition that the value of the project extends beyond the final artwork, indicating mature metacognitive awareness
Use of professional-standard screenshots (e.g., Procreate interface) to evidence growth in technical skills and adaptability
Reflective description of overcoming technical challenges, highlighting your inquirer profile
weaknesses: Reflections lack specific data or examples (e.g., survey percentages, exact before-and-after comparisons) to illustrate how criteria were met or exceeded
General statements about risk-taking and blending transitions need concrete incidents and links back to initial success criteria
Evaluation table and calendar visualizations would be stronger if annotated with actual feedback data and notes on mid-project adjustments
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective section demonstrates strong self-awareness and an ability to articulate how the project has contributed to your growth as an inquirer and a digital artist. To elevate this further, ground your evaluations in specific evidenceâsuch as numerical feedback, detailed comparisons to your success criteria, and examples of risk-taking experimentsâto show exactly how your learning evolved and how adjustments shaped the final outcome.

annotations.comment: Including a screenshot of your digital art application concretely illustrates how you applied communication skills to share your visual ideas.
Handwritten mentor notes serve as strong primary evidence of your research and collaboration ATL skills in action.
Your reflection on learning impact is insightful; consider adding specific examples or data to illustrate how these skills improved your final product.

Your opening paragraph trails off without clearly stating your learning goal. Consider explicitly articulating a specific, measurable learning goal linked to your personal interest at the start of your planning section.
The diagram outlines three learning goals but lacks measurable success criteria. Add specific indicators for each goal, such as target software proficiency levels or completion milestones.
Your SMART analysis for the learning goal clearly defines specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-based criteria. This strengthens your planning by providing clear success markers.
You succinctly state your learning goal and product outcome in this section. This clear articulation guides the rest of your planning and aligns your intentions with concrete objectives.
The SMART analysis for your product goal is detailed and aligned with your objectives, offering measurable milestones and relevance to your target audience.
Your research source table identifies relevant primary and secondary sources, but it lacks a timeline or sequencing. Map each source to specific research phases to enhance feasibility.
The success-criteria rubric is comprehensive but would benefit from linking each criterion to the resources or tools you plan to use, ensuring a clear path to achievement.
This Gantt chart provides a comprehensive, long-term overview of your project tasks and deadlines, demonstrating strong sequencing and resource allocation.
The December action plan shows completion but lacks notes on post-exhibition adjustments. Add reflection on how feedback will shape your final revisions.
The weekly schedule Gantt chart shows self-management efforts, but clarify how it aligns with your long-term milestones for more cohesive planning.
The color-coded task table outlines roles effectively, but the legend context is minimal. Briefly explain each colorâs scheduling logic to improve clarity in your plan.
While you note digital art as your communication medium, explicitly link how this application improved your learning progress by citing a specific outcome from this use.
The photo of peer interaction demonstrates effective negotiation and feedback gathering, highlighting your application of communication ATL skills.
Your description of self-management through Gantt and monthly planning clearly demonstrates the application of organization ATL skills.
The Procreate interface screenshot effectively evidences your transfer to professional tools and growth in digital art skills.
You mention peer and teacher feedback, but providing a concrete example of a suggestion you implemented would better showcase the impact of these interactions.
This screenshot exemplifies your inquirer profile as you navigated technical challenges, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving.
Your insight that the project value extends beyond the final product demonstrates mature reflective thinking on growth and learning processes.
You identify being a risk-taker, yet you could strengthen this by detailing a specific experiment and its positive outcome on your artwork.
When discussing blending transitions, tie your reflection back to your initial success criteria for narrative clarity to show how you met or revised expectations.
Your evaluation table is comprehensive; to deepen reflection, reference specific survey percentages or product imagery alongside each justification.
Your calendar visualization supports a balanced profile; annotate how you adjusted commitments mid-month to reflect real-world changes.
